# PDF Dark Mode Converter

> A free, open-source tool that converts any PDF to dark mode instantly in your browser. No uploads, no server, fully offline and private.

## What it does
- Converts PDF files to dark mode for easier reading at night
- Inverts PDF colors (separate tool at /invert-pdf-colors/)
- Runs entirely client-side in the browser using JavaScript
- No data ever leaves the user's device
- Up to 10x faster than similar tools using GPU-accelerated color transforms

## Features
- 16 built-in dark themes: Classic, Slate, Claude Warm, ChatGPT Cool, Sepia, Midnight Blue, Forest Green, Solarized, Nord, Dracula, Gruvbox, Monokai, Tokyo Night, Rosé Pine, Cobalt, plus Invert (Negative)
- Custom theme with hex color picker and preset swatches
- Dark and light mode output (light-to-dark and dark-to-light)
- Adjustable resolution (0.5x-4x) and JPEG quality (50-100%)
- Selectable text layer preserved in converted PDFs
- Image preservation toggle (keeps photos intact)
- Batch conversion (multiple PDFs at once)
- Drag and drop or file picker
- Automatic download of converted PDF
- Works offline after first load

## Pages
- Main converter: /
- Invert PDF Colors tool: /invert-pdf-colors/
- Blog with 14 guides: /blog/
- About: /about/
- Changelog: /changelog/
- Privacy Policy: /privacy/
- Terms of Service: /terms/
- Contact: /contact/

## Languages
Available in 10 languages: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Chinese, Korean, Japanese, Russian

## Technical details
- Uses pdf.js for rendering and pdf-lib for PDF assembly
- GPU-accelerated via SVG feColorMatrix filter
- JPEG encoding for fast page processing
- Concurrent page processing with parallel workers
- Static site hosted on GitHub Pages, no server-side code

## URL
https://bratuka.dev/pdf-dark-mode-converter/

## Source
https://github.com/BrAtUkA/pdf-dark-mode-converter
